stem: The equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has a characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \) with a repeated (double) root. What is that repeated root?
solution: The characteristic equation is \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \), which factors as \( (r - [[p]])^{2} = 0 \) since the discriminant is zero. The repeated root is \( r = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This flips the sign of the root.
This reports \( -([[b]]) = 2([[p]]) \), the sum of the two equal roots.
This uses the coefficient \( [[b]] \) directly as the root.
This uses the constant term \( [[c]] = [[p]]^{2} \) as the root.
This is one more than the root.
This is one less than the root.
This is two more than the root.

stem: For what value of \( c \) does the equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + c\,y = 0 \) have a repeated (double) characteristic root?
solution: A repeated root occurs exactly when the discriminant vanishes: \( ([[b]])^{2} - 4c = 0 \), so \( c = \dfrac{([[b]])^{2}}{4} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by 4 in the discriminant condition.
This reports half of \( b \) instead of a quarter of \( b^{2} \).
This has the wrong sign.
This divides by 2 instead of 4.
This uses \( b \) itself instead of \( b^{2}/4 \).
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: Solve the initial value problem \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \), \( y(0) = [[y0]] \), \( y'(0) = [[v0]] \) (a repeated-root case), and evaluate \( y([[t0]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( v_0 \) directly as \( C_2 \), forgetting to subtract \( r\,y_0 \).
This drops the second term of the repeated-root solution entirely.
This drops the first term of the repeated-root solution entirely.
This flips the sign of the \( C_2 t \) term.
This flips the sign in the exponential, giving decay instead of growth or vice versa.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: For a repeated characteristic root \( r = [[r]] \), the second linearly independent solution of the ODE is \( y = t\,e^{[[r]]t} \). What is \( y''([[t0]]) \)?
distractor_rationales: This reports \( y \) itself rather than \( y'' \).
This reports (part of) \( y' \) rather than the full second derivative.
This reports \( y' \) rather than \( y'' \).
This omits the \( 2r \) term that arises from differentiating the product rule twice.
This omits the \( r^{2}t \) term, keeping only the constant part of \( y'' \).
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.
